content.title: Username & Password
content.content:
Authentication is an important part of every software. Sapphire is not an exception

## Install

To add simple Username-Password authentication to your project, run:

```shell
sapphire-cms package install simple-auth
```

or 

```shell
scms pkg i simple-auth
```

## Configuration

Configure the administrator credentials in the `sapphire-cms.config.yaml` file as shown below.

**Security warning**

Never store credentials in plain text within your configuration files. This introduces serious security risks, especially if your CMS is exposed to the internet.
Always use environment variables or platform-specific secrets to manage sensitive data.


```yaml
# ./sapphire-cms.config.yaml

config:
  modules:
    - module: simple-auth
      config:
        username: ${env.SAPPHIRE_USERNAME}
        password: ${env.SAPPHIRE_PASSWORD}

layers:
  security: '@simple-auth'
```

**Notes:**

- `${env.SAPPHIRE_USERNAME}` and `${env.SAPPHIRE_PASSWORD}` are resolved at runtime from environment variables.
- Make sure these variables are properly configured in your hosting platform (cloud provider or local environment).

### Module parameters

| Parameter | Type   | Mandatory | Description              |
| --------- | ------ | --------- | ------------------------ |
| username  | string | yes       | Administrators username. |
| password  | string | yes       | Administrators password. |
